scope.summary:
Goal: let users drag CKC (Atelier/KERNEL-005) media items, characters, and moodboards into a native note or canvas pane as embedded loom:// references, and route any note selection or document to a Stage pane. Expected diff shape: (a) a new drag-source on the AtelierSidePanel widget that emits a DragPayload::AtelierRef; (b) a drop-target in the rich-text editor that converts a dropped AtelierRef into an embed node (similar to how React LoomTransclusionView.tsx inserts a loomTransclusion TipTap node); (c) a drop-target in the canvas pane that places a canvas node pinned to the atelier item; (d) a 'Route to Stage' command on the InteractionBus that takes the current selection or document and opens/focuses the Stage pane with that content; (e) integration tests using a real PG backend to assert the embed survives a save/reload cycle.

Files to CREATE:
  - src/frontend/shared/drag_payload.rs  — defines DragPayload enum: AtelierRef { item_id: String, item_kind: AtelierItemKind, label: String, loom_block_id: Option<String> }, LoomBlockRef { block_id: String, workspace_id: String }, PlainText(String). Also defines AtelierItemKind enum: Media | Character | Moodboard.
  - src/frontend/widgets/atelier_side_panel.rs  — an egui side-panel listing AtelierIntakeBatch items and AtelierCommandCorpus entries fetched from the existing backend (GET /atelier/intake/batches, GET /atelier/command-corpus). Each item row supports egui drag-source: on drag-start stores DragPayload::AtelierRef in ctx.data_mut. Rows display thumbnail path + label. AccessKit: panel container author_id="atelier-side-panel", role=List; each row author_id="atelier-item-{item_id}", role=ListItem, actions=[Press, StartDrag].

Files to EDIT:
  - src/frontend/shared/mod.rs  — add `pub mod drag_payload;`
  - src/frontend/widgets/mod.rs  — add `pub mod atelier_side_panel;`
  - src/frontend/panes/rich_text_editor.rs  — add an egui drop-target zone over the editor area. When a drop is received from ctx.data(|d| d.get_temp::<DragPayload>(...)), match on AtelierRef: insert an embed node { node_type: 'atelierEmbed', item_id, item_kind, label, loom_block_id } into the document tree at the caret position. Serialize as a custom ProseMirror-style block in the rich doc JSON. On save, the block is stored as part of content_json and round-trips through saveRichDocument.
  - src/frontend/panes/canvas_pane.rs  — add drop-target at canvas level. On AtelierRef drop at screen position: call POST /canvas/{canvas_id}/graph with a new CanvasNodeInput { kind: 'atelier_ref', atelier_item_id, x, y, width, height, label }. Backend stores it; reload canvas graph. The placed node's header shows AtelierItemKind badge + label + loom:// chip if loom_block_id is Some.
  - src/frontend/panes/stage_pane.rs  — add or extend a StagePane widget. The Stage pane accepts a StageContent enum: Document(RichDocLoad) | Selection(String, String) | AtelierItem(AtelierRef). Register a 'route-to-stage' command on the InteractionBus (MT-031): args = StageRouteArgs { content: StageContent }; the command opens the Stage pane tile (via egui_tiles) and sets its content.
  - src/frontend/shared/interaction_bus.rs  — add route_to_stage(content: StageContent) helper and 'route-to-stage' command descriptor.

Step-by-step implementation notes:
  1. egui drag-source: call ui.dnd_drag_source(egui::Id::new(item_id), payload, |ui| { ui.label(&row.label) }). The payload is DragPayload::AtelierRef. This is egui 0.28+ API; confirm version in Cargo.toml.
  2. egui drop-target in rich-text pane: call let response = ui.dnd_drop_zone::<DragPayload, _>(egui::Frame::none(), |ui| { ... }); then check response.1 (the dropped payload) in each frame.
  3. Embed node in rich doc JSON: define a custom block type "atelier_embed" in the document schema. Store as a JSONContent object node with attrs: { item_id, item_kind, label, loom_block_id }. The rich-text editor renders it as a small card widget inline (thumbnail if available, else text label with badge).
  4. Canvas node creation for atelier drop: map screen position (egui Pos2) to canvas coordinates. Call updateCanvasGraph (api.ts ~1719: PUT /canvas/{id}/graph body { nodes: [{ kind, label, x, y, width, height, atelier_item_id }], edges: [] }) using the Rust IPC client. Then reload via GET /canvas/{id}.
  5. Route to Stage: the Stage pane is an egui_tiles tile in the docking layout. 'route-to-stage' command handler: find the Stage tile id in the tree, call tree.set_active_tile(stage_tile_id), then set stage_pane.content = StageContent::from(args). If Stage tile does not exist yet, insert it as a new floating pane.
  6. Right-click context menu on rich-text selection: show 'Route to Stage' menu item. This calls bus.dispatch_command("route-to-stage", StageRouteArgs::Selection(selected_text, document_id)).
  7. Right-click context menu on canvas node: show 'Route to Stage' item that routes the node's document or atelier item.
  8. AtelierSidePanel data loading: call GET /atelier/intake/batches + GET /atelier/command-corpus on mount. Display in two sections: 'Media / Characters / Moodboards' (from intake items) and 'Command Corpus'. Refresh button fires a reload.
  9. AccessKit: atelier_side_panel rows have actions=[Press, StartDrag]; the Stage pane container has author_id="stage-pane", role=Region.
  10. Tests: src/frontend/tests/ckc_embed_tests.rs — (a) unit: DragPayload::AtelierRef serializes and deserializes correctly; (b) integration (real PG): create a canvas, POST /canvas/{id}/graph with an atelier_ref node, reload, assert node appears with correct atelier_item_id; (c) integration: create a rich doc, insert an atelier_embed block, saveRichDocument, reload, assert the atelier_embed block survives round-trip; (d) kittest: simulate drag from atelier panel, drop on canvas, assert canvas node count increases by 1.

Dependencies / unblock conditions:
  - MT-031 (InteractionBus, command registration) merged.
  - MT-032 (LoomBlockAddr, loom:// URI) merged — needed for loom_block_id in AtelierRef.
  - MT-013 (rich-text embed NodeView infrastructure) merged — needed so the atelier_embed block has a renderer in the rich-text pane.
  - egui 0.28+ for dnd_drag_source / dnd_drop_zone API; confirm version in Cargo.toml.

Risk if missed: CKC<->editor interconnection is one of the four named interconnection edges in the packet contract. Without this MT, dragging a character or moodboard into a note is impossible, breaking the 'melt-together' proof in MT-046.
acceptance_criteria: Dragging an item from the AtelierSidePanel and dropping it onto the rich-text editor inserts an 'atelier_embed' block at the caret position — proven by kittest drag-and-drop simulation.
The 'atelier_embed' block survives a saveRichDocument + loadRichDocument round-trip with all attrs (item_id, item_kind, label) intact — proven by integration test against real PG.

Dragging an item from AtelierSidePanel and dropping onto the canvas creates a new canvas node with atelier_item_id set — proven by integration test: POST /canvas graph, reload, assert node count and atelier_item_id.

The 'Route to Stage' command, dispatched from the context menu on a rich-text selection, opens the Stage pane and displays the selected text — proven by kittest: dispatch command, assert stage-pane AccessKit node visible with correct content.
AtelierSidePanel loads batches and corpus entries from the real backend on mount (no mocks) — proven by integration test asserting at least one item in the list when a batch exists.
AccessKit nodes: 'atelier-side-panel' (List), at least one 'atelier-item-{id}' (ListItem with StartDrag action), 'stage-pane' (Region) — proven by AccessKit tree dump.
cargo test -p handshake_native_frontend ckc_embed -- --nocapture passes with no failures.
proof_targets: cargo test -p handshake_native_frontend ckc_embed -- --nocapture  =>  all PASS
kittest: simulate drag from 'atelier-item-{id}', drop on rich-text editor, assert 'atelier_embed' node present in document tree
integration test (real PG): POST canvas graph with atelier_ref node, reload canvas, assert node with atelier_item_id exists
integration test (real PG): insert atelier_embed in rich doc, save, reload, assert block survives
kittest: right-click selection, click 'Route to Stage', assert 'stage-pane' AccessKit node visible
implementation_notes: egui 0.29 (as of 2025) has egui::DragAndDrop for payload-typed drag. Check the locked egui version in Cargo.toml. If < 0.28, implement drag via egui::memory drag_id pattern: on mouse-down store payload, on mouse-up check hovering pane and apply.

AtelierSidePanel uses GET /atelier/intake/batches (api.ts line 2818 listAtelierIntakeBatches) and GET /atelier/command-corpus (api.ts line 2849 listAtelierCommandCorpus); these are the only two endpoints needed for the item list.

Stage pane: egui_tiles supports inserting a new tile dynamically. Keep a stage_tile_id: Option<egui_tiles::TileId> in app state; if None, call tiles.insert_pane(Box::new(StagePane::default())) and record the id.
Route to Stage for AtelierItem: pass the item_id; Stage pane calls GET /atelier/intake/batches (or a per-item endpoint if available) to load item metadata on demand.

React reference: AtelierPanel.tsx uses getAtelierIntakeItems(batchId) and listAtelierCommandCorpus() to populate its sections — mirror this in AtelierSidePanel. AtelierCollaborationPanel.tsx shows the collaboration panel pattern.
ports_from_react: app/src/components/AtelierPanel.tsx — getAtelierOverview, listAtelierIntakeBatches, getAtelierIntakeItems, listAtelierCommandCorpus, openAtelierIntakeBatch patterns
app/src/components/AtelierCollaborationPanel.tsx — collaboration panel layout and section structure
app/src/components/LoomTransclusionView.tsx — embed node insertion pattern (loomTransclusion node in TipTap; mirror as atelier_embed in native block model)
binds_backend_api: PUT /canvas/{canvas_id}/graph  =>  api.ts updateCanvasGraph  =>  body CanvasNodeInput[] + CanvasEdgeInput[]  =>  adds canvas node
GET /canvas/{canvas_id}  =>  api.ts getCanvas  =>  returns CanvasWithGraph (reloads nodes after create)
POST/PUT /knowledge/documents/{doc_id}  =>  api.ts saveRichDocument  =>  persists atelier_embed block in content_json

risks: egui DnD API version mismatch: dnd_drag_source / dnd_drop_zone may not exist in the locked egui version; fallback to manual drag state is more complex and untested.
atelier_embed block schema conflict: if the rich-text block schema does not declare the 'atelier_embed' node type, the document will fail to save or reload, silently dropping the embed.
Canvas node POST may fail if atelier_item_id is not a valid field in the backend CanvasNodeInput schema; must inspect the actual backend type before assuming the field exists.
Stage pane tile insertion race: if the Stage pane tile is inserted while egui_tiles is in the middle of a layout pass, the tile may not render until the next frame, causing a flash.
AtelierSidePanel load on mount blocks: if /atelier/intake/batches is slow, the panel shows empty with no loading indicator, confusing the user.
minimum_controls: Check egui version in Cargo.toml before using dnd_drag_source; if < 0.28 document the fallback approach in implementation_notes and implement the manual drag-id pattern.
Declare 'atelier_embed' as an explicit allowed node type in the rich-text document schema validator; reject unknown node types at save time with a typed error.
Inspect backend src/backend/handshake_core/src for CanvasNodeInput before assuming atelier_item_id field; if absent, raise as a typed backend blocker and use the node's label field as a workaround.
Stage pane insertion: set stage_tile_id only after egui_tiles confirms the tile is in the tree (check tiles.contains(id)); if not yet in tree, re-insert next frame.
Show a spinner in AtelierSidePanel during load; display error text on fetch failure; never show a blank panel without explanation.
